ffmpeg_encoder.h(3) Library Functions Manual ffmpeg_encoder.h(3)

NAME

ffmpeg_encoder.h - FFmpeg video/image file encoder — codec selected from file extension.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct ffmpeg_encoder_s ffmpeg_encoder_t

Functions


asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_create (const char *output_path, int width_px, int height_px, int fps, ffmpeg_encoder_t **out)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, const uint8_t *rgb, int pitch, uint64_t captured_ns)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, const float *samples, int num_samples)
void ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_destroy (ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc)

Detailed Description

FFmpeg video/image file encoder — codec selected from file extension.

Overview

The FFmpeg encoder provides a simple interface for encoding video frames or images to disk. The encoder automatically detects the output format and codec from the file extension, handling all necessary color space conversions and encoder configuration.

Input format: RGB24 (8-bit per channel, 24-bit per pixel) Output: File on disk with automatic format/codec detection

Supported Formats & Codecs

Extension Container Video Codec Audio Codec Pixel Format Use Case .mp4, .mov MP4 H.264 AAC YUV420P General streaming, web playback .mkv Matroska H.264 AAC YUV420P High-quality archival, flexible codecs .webm WebM VP9 Opus YUV420P Web-optimized streaming, best compression .avi AVI MPEG-4 PCM YUV420P Legacy format, older systems .flv Flash H.264 AAC YUV420P Flash video, streaming history .ogv, .ogg Ogg Theora Vorbis YUV420P Free/open-source streaming .3gp 3GP H.264 AAC YUV420P Mobile devices, low bitrate .gif GIF GIF None Palette Animated clips, social media .png Image PNG None RGB24 Lossless still images, single frame .jpg, .jpeg Image MJPEG None YUVJ420P Lossy still images, single frame

Default format: MP4 (if extension not recognized) Audio: Automatically selected based on container; image formats are audio-free

Encoder Options & Quality Tradeoffs

Automatic Bitrate Calculation:

  • Based on resolution: ~1 Mbps per megapixel
  • Range: 500 kbps (minimum) to 5000 kbps (maximum)
  • Formula: bitrate_kbps = (width × height) / 1024, clamped to [500, 5000]
  • Provides reasonable quality for most use cases without manual tuning

Color Space Conversion:

  • Input: RGB24 (24-bit color, standard in graphics/video capture)
  • Conversion method: libswscale with SWS_BICUBIC algorithm
  • SWS_BICUBIC: Bicubic interpolation offering good quality-to-speed balance
  • Pixel format selection:
•
YUV420P: Video formats (H.264, VP9, MPEG-4) — standard ITU-R BT.601 conversion
•
YUVJ420P: JPEG-based formats (MJPEG) — uses full-range YUV (0-255)
•
RGB24: Lossless formats (PNG, GIF) — preserves exact RGB values

Quality vs. Performance:

  • H.264: Best compression, moderate speed (suitable for streaming)
  • VP9: Better quality/size ratio, slower encoding (smaller files)
  • PNG: Lossless, larger files, very slow encoding (use for critical frames)
  • GIF: Lossless animation, severely limited palette (max 256 colors)
  • MJPEG: Fast single-frame capture, reasonable compression

Single-Frame Image Handling:

  • PNG and JPEG formats (.png, .jpg, .jpeg) output only one frame
  • Multiple ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame() calls write subsequent frames to the same file
  • Useful for snapshots or periodic frame capture

Usage Examples

Encode video to MP4 (default format):

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_create("output.mp4", 1920, 1080, 30, &enc);
if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    // Handle error
}
// Write frames (RGB24 format, pitch in bytes per row)
uint8_t *frame_rgb = get_frame_data();
int pitch = 1920 * 3;  // 1920 pixels × 3 bytes per pixel
ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame_rgb, pitch);
// Cleanup and finalize file
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);

High-quality WebM encoding (better compression than H.264):

// Simply use .webm extension
ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("output.webm", 1920, 1080, 24, &enc);
// ... write frames ...
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);

Capture single frame to PNG (lossless snapshot):

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("snapshot.png", 1920, 1080, 1, &enc);
uint8_t *frame = capture_frame();
ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame, 1920 * 3);
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);  // Finalizes and closes file

Animated GIF from frame sequence:

ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc;
ffmpeg_encoder_create("animation.gif", 640, 480, 10, &enc);  // 10 FPS
for (int i = 0; i < num_frames; i++) {


    uint8_t *frame = get_frame(i);


    ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(enc, frame, 640 * 3);
}
ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(enc);  // Finalizes GIF with palette

Common Pitfalls & Best Practices

1. Pitch/Stride Parameter:

  • pitch is the byte offset from one row to the next
  • For tightly-packed RGB: pitch = width × 3
  • For aligned/padded buffers: use actual row size (may be larger)
  • Must match the actual memory layout of the input buffer

2. Memory Management:

  • Input frame data is NOT owned by encoder — caller must manage
  • Output file is owned by encoder and written/closed by ffmpeg_encoder_destroy()
  • All resources freed in ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() — always call it

3. Error Recovery:

  • Encoder is NOT usable after any error from ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame()
  • Must call ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() to clean up and finalize file
  • If file operations fail, file may be partially written

4. File Extension Sensitivity:

  • Extension matching is case-sensitive on Unix-like systems
  • Use lowercase extensions (.mp4 not .MP4) for maximum compatibility
  • Unknown extension defaults to MP4 H.264

5. Platform Support:

  • This encoder is NOT AVAILABLE on Windows (_WIN32)
  • Only POSIX systems (Linux, macOS) are supported

Performance Considerations

Encoding Speed (relative):

  • Fastest: GIF, MJPEG (lossless single-frame)
  • Fast: H.264 with default bitrate
  • Medium: VP9 (better compression at cost of speed)
  • Slow: PNG (lossless color preservation)

File Size (for 1080p @ 30fps, 10 seconds):

  • MP4 H.264: ~50-100 MB (depends on bitrate)
  • WebM VP9: ~30-60 MB (better compression)
  • AVI MPEG-4: ~100-150 MB (less efficient compression)
  • PNG: ~100+ MB per frame (lossless, single frame only)
  • GIF: ~200+ MB for 300 frames (palette limited)

Bitrate Impact on Quality:

  • Default bitrate is calculated for acceptable quality at various resolutions
  • For critical content (e.g., document sharing), consider PNG for single frames
  • For streaming, MP4 H.264 offers best balance of quality, compatibility, and size
  • VP9 recommended for highest quality requirements when file size isn't critical

Definition in file ffmpeg_encoder.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct ffmpeg_encoder_s ffmpeg_encoder_t

Definition at line 162 of file ffmpeg_encoder.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_create (const char * output_path, int width_px, int height_px, int fps, ffmpeg_encoder_t ** out)

Create and initialize a video/image encoder.

The output format and codec are automatically determined from the file extension. See Supported Formats & Codecs for supported formats.

Bitrate: Automatically calculated from resolution (~1 Mbps per megapixel, clamped to 500-5000 kbps).

Color Space: Input is expected in RGB24 format. Internal conversion to the appropriate output pixel format (YUV420P for video, RGB24 for PNG, YUVJ420P for JPEG) is handled automatically.

Parameters

output_path Path to the output file (extension determines format and codec)
width_px Video width in pixels (must be > 0)
height_px Video height in pixels (must be > 0)
fps Frames per second (must be > 0)
out Pointer to encoder handle (set on success)

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise

Note

The encoder acquires resources (file handles, memory buffers) that must be released with ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(). Failure to do so may leak file handles and memory.

See also

ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame, ffmpeg_encoder_destroy, ffmpeg_encoder_formats

Definition at line 336 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.

337                                                                 {
338   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_CREATE] START: output=%s, %dx%d @ %dfps", output_path, width_px, height_px, fps);
339 
340   if (!output_path || !out || width_px <= 0 || height_px <= 0)
341     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ffmpeg_encoder_create: invalid parameters");
342 
343   // Use default FPS if not specified (0 from config means "use default")
344   if (fps <= 0) {
345     fps = 60;
346     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: fps was 0, using default 60");
347   }
348 
349   ffmpeg_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*enc), ffmpeg_encoder_t *);
350   enc->width_px = width_px;
351   enc->height_px = height_px;
352 
353   // For snapshot mode, calculate output FPS based on input FPS and snapshot duration
354   // If we capture for N seconds at input_fps, we'll get ~(N * input_fps) frames
355   // Output duration should be snapshot_delay seconds
356   // So output_fps = (input_fps * snapshot_delay) / snapshot_delay = input_fps
357   // EXCEPT: if the input was probed, fps parameter is the actual input fps
358   // Then output_fps should equal input_fps to make frame durations sum correctly
359   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
360   double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
361   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: snapshot_mode=%d, snapshot_delay=%.1f, input_fps=%d",
362             snapshot_mode, snapshot_delay, fps);
363 
364   if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
365     // For snapshot mode: if we capture at input_fps for snapshot_delay seconds,
366     // we get approximately (input_fps * snapshot_delay) frames
367     // To make output span exactly snapshot_delay seconds:
368     // output_fps = frame_count / snapshot_delay = (input_fps * snapshot_delay) / snapshot_delay = input_fps
369     // So we should use input_fps as output_fps!
370     // Example: 30fps input for 3s → ~90 frames → output at 30fps → 90/30 = 3 seconds ✓
371 
372     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode snapshot_delay=%.1f, input_fps=%d",
373              snapshot_delay, fps);
374     log_info("  → Will estimate ~%.0f frames, output FPS=%d makes durations sum to %.1f seconds",
375              fps * snapshot_delay, fps, snapshot_delay);
376   }
377 
378   enc->fps = fps;
379 
380   // Snapshot mode frame distribution setup
381   enc->estimated_frame_count = 0;
382   enc->estimated_frame_duration = 0;
383   if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0 && fps > 0) {
384     // Estimate how many frames we'll capture
385     enc->estimated_frame_count = (int)(fps * snapshot_delay + 0.5);
386     // Each frame should have uniform duration in stream time_base (1/90000 for MP4)
387     // frame_duration = total_duration / estimated_frame_count
388     //                = snapshot_delay seconds * time_base.den / estimated_frame_count
389     // We'll set this to stream->time_base later after it's initialized
390     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode ENABLED - estimated_frame_count=%d (fps=%d * snapshot_delay=%.2f)",
391               enc->estimated_frame_count, fps, snapshot_delay);
392   } else {
393     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Snapshot mode disabled or invalid - snapshot_mode=%d, snapshot_delay=%.2f, fps=%d",
394              snapshot_mode, snapshot_delay, fps);
395   }
396   enc->is_stdout_pipe = (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) ? 1 : 0;
397 
398   // For codec/format detection, use output_path unless it's "-" (stdout)
399   // For stdout, use "fake.mp4" to default to MP4 format
400   const char *detection_path = output_path;
401   if (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) {
402     detection_path = "fake.mp4"; // Fake path for format detection when piping to stdout
403     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Using MP4 format for stdout piping");
404   }
405 
406   const char *codec_name = NULL, *format_name = NULL;
407   get_codec_from_extension(detection_path, &codec_name, &format_name, &enc->is_image, &enc->target_pix_fmt);
408 
409   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: %s (%s, %dx%d @ %dfps, %s)", output_path, codec_name, width_px, height_px, fps,
410             enc->is_image ? "image" : "video");
411 
412   // Allocate output media context
413   int ret = avformat_alloc_output_context2(&enc->fmt_ctx, NULL, format_name, output_path);
414   if (ret < 0) {
415     SAFE_FREE(enc);
416     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_alloc_output_context2 failed");
417   }
418 
419   // Find encoder (with fallback for FFmpeg builds without libx264)
420   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name(codec_name);
421   if (!codec && strcmp(codec_name, "libx264") == 0) {
422     // libx264 not available - try built-in H.264 encoders
423     static const char *h264_fallbacks[] = {"libopenh264", "h264_mf", NULL};
424     for (int i = 0; h264_fallbacks[i] && !codec; i++) {
425       codec = avcodec_find_encoder_by_name(h264_fallbacks[i]);
426     }
427     if (!codec) {
428       // Last resort: ask FFmpeg for any H.264 encoder
429       codec = avcodec_find_encoder(AV_CODEC_ID_H264);
430     }
431     if (codec) {
432       log_info("ffmpeg: using fallback H.264 encoder '%s' (libx264 not available)", codec->name);
433     }
434   }
435   if (!codec) {
436     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
437     SAFE_FREE(enc);
438     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: encoder '%s' not found", codec_name);
439   }
440 
441   // Create new stream
442   enc->stream = avformat_new_stream(enc->fmt_ctx, codec);
443   if (!enc->stream) {
444     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
445     SAFE_FREE(enc);
446     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_new_stream failed");
447   }
448 
449   // Allocate codec context
450   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
451   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
452     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
453     SAFE_FREE(enc);
454     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_alloc_context3 failed");
455   }
456 
457   // Set codec parameters
458   enc->codec_ctx->width = width_px;
459   enc->codec_ctx->height = height_px;
460   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = enc->target_pix_fmt;
461   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, fps};
462   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){fps, 1};
463 
464   // Set bitrate for high quality (music-level quality)
465   // Use ~5-10 Mbps per megapixel for visually lossless quality
466   int bitrate = (width_px * height_px * 10) / 1024; // higher quality estimate in kbps
467   if (bitrate < 4000)
468     bitrate = 4000; // Minimum 4000 kbps for quality
469   if (bitrate > 50000)
470     bitrate = 50000; // Cap at 50 Mbps max
471   enc->codec_ctx->bit_rate = bitrate * 1000;
472   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: bitrate set to %d kbps for %dx%d", bitrate, width_px, height_px);
473 
474   // Configure codec-specific options for quality
475   AVDictionary *codec_opts = NULL;
476   if (strcmp(codec_name, "libx264") == 0) {
477     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "preset", "ultrafast", 0);
478     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "crf", "28", 0);
479     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: x264 preset=ultrafast crf=28");
480   } else if (strcmp(codec_name, "libx265") == 0) {
481     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "preset", "ultrafast", 0);
482     av_dict_set(&codec_opts, "crf", "32", 0);
483     // x265 tag must be hvc1 for MP4 container compatibility (Apple/browser playback)
484     enc->stream->codecpar->codec_tag = MKTAG('h', 'v', 'c', '1');
485     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder: x265 preset=ultrafast crf=32");
486   }
487 
488   // Open codec (capture libx264 startup logs)
489   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
490     ret = avcodec_open2(enc->codec_ctx, codec, &codec_opts);
491   });
492   av_dict_free(&codec_opts);
493   if (ret < 0) {
494     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
495     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
496     SAFE_FREE(enc);
497     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_open2 failed");
498   }
499 
500   // Copy codec parameters to stream
501   ret = avcodec_parameters_from_context(enc->stream->codecpar, enc->codec_ctx);
502   if (ret < 0) {
503     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
504     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
505     SAFE_FREE(enc);
506     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avcodec_parameters_from_context failed");
507   }
508 
509   // Muxer options for proper MP4 container structure
510   AVDictionary *opts = NULL;
511 
512   // Muxer flags configuration
513   // For stdout/non-seekable output, use fragmented MP4 (frag_keyframe)
514   // For regular files, use faststart (moov at front for streaming)
515   bool is_stdout = (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0);
516   if (is_stdout) {
517     // Use fragmented MP4 for stdout - starts a new fragment at each video keyframe
518     // This allows streaming without requiring file seeking (FFmpeg docs recommended)
519     av_dict_set(&opts, "movflags", "+frag_keyframe", 0);
520     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Using fragmented MP4 (+frag_keyframe) for stdout output");
521   } else {
522     // Move moov atom to the front (faststart) - helps with streaming and player compatibility
523     av_dict_set(&opts, "movflags", "faststart", 0);
524   }
525 
526   if (enc->is_image) {
527     // Use -update flag for single frame output
528     av_dict_set(&opts, "update", "1", 0);
529   }
530 
531   // Allocate frames
532   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
533   enc->frame_encoded = av_frame_alloc();
534   enc->pkt = av_packet_alloc();
535   if (!enc->frame || !enc->frame_encoded || !enc->pkt) {
536     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
537     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
538     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
539     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
540     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
541     SAFE_FREE(enc);
542     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: frame allocation failed");
543   }
544 
545   // Set frame properties for target pixel format
546   enc->frame_encoded->format = enc->target_pix_fmt;
547   enc->frame_encoded->width = width_px;
548   enc->frame_encoded->height = height_px;
549   ret = av_frame_get_buffer(enc->frame_encoded, 32);
550   if (ret < 0) {
551     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
552     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
553     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
554     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
555     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
556     SAFE_FREE(enc);
557     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: av_frame_get_buffer failed");
558   }
559 
560   // Create SWS context for RGB → target format conversion
561   LOG_IO("swscaler", {
562     enc->sws_ctx = sws_getContext(width_px, height_px, AV_PIX_FMT_RGB24, width_px, height_px, enc->target_pix_fmt,
563                                   SWS_BICUBIC, NULL, NULL, NULL);
564   });
565   if (!enc->sws_ctx) {
566     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
567     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
568     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
569     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
570     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
571     SAFE_FREE(enc);
572     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: sws_getContext failed");
573   }
574 
575   // Initialize audio stream if supported
576   encoder_init_audio_stream(enc, output_path);
577 
578   // Open output file or stdout
579   if (!(enc->fmt_ctx->oformat->flags & AVFMT_NOFILE)) {
580     // For stdout output, use "pipe:1" (FFmpeg's standard for writing to stdout)
581     const char *open_path = output_path;
582     if (output_path && strcmp(output_path, "-") == 0) {
583       open_path = "pipe:1"; // Use pipe:1 for stdout
584       log_debug("ffmpeg: Redirecting '-' to 'pipe:1' for stdout output");
585     }
586     ret = avio_open(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb, open_path, AVIO_FLAG_WRITE);
587     if (ret < 0) {
588       sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
589       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
590       av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
591       av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
592       avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
593       avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
594       SAFE_FREE(enc);
595       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avio_open failed for '%s'", open_path);
596     }
597   }
598 
599   // Write header (capture FFmpeg format muxer logs)
600   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
601     ret = avformat_write_header(enc->fmt_ctx, &opts);
602   });
603   av_dict_free(&opts); // Free options after use
604   if (ret < 0) {
605     avio_closep(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
606     sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
607     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
608     av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
609     av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
610     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
611     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
612     SAFE_FREE(enc);
613     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "ffmpeg: avformat_write_header failed");
614   }
615 
616   // Now that stream->time_base is set, calculate estimated frame duration for snapshot mode
617   bool snapshot_mode_after_header = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
618   double snapshot_delay_after_header = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
619   if (snapshot_mode_after_header && enc->estimated_frame_count > 0 && snapshot_delay_after_header > 0) {
620     // Each frame should span: snapshot_delay / estimated_frame_count seconds
621     // In stream time_base units: (snapshot_delay * time_base.den) / estimated_frame_count
622     enc->estimated_frame_duration =
623         (int64_t)((snapshot_delay_after_header * (double)enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->estimated_frame_count);
624     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_create: Calculated estimated_frame_duration=%lld time_base units "
625               "for snapshot mode (snapshot_delay=%.2f, estimated_frames=%d, time_base=%d/%d)",
626               (long long)enc->estimated_frame_duration, snapshot_delay_after_header, enc->estimated_frame_count,
627               enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den);
628   }
629 
630   enc->frame_count = 0;
631   enc->video_pts = 0;
632   enc->snapshot_actual_duration = 0.0;
633   enc->snapshot_elapsed_ns = 0;
634   enc->first_frame_captured_ns = 0;  // Initialize first frame timestamp
635   *out = enc;
636 
637   /* Register encoder with named registry */
638   NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_ENCODER(enc, output_path, NULL);
639 
640   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_CREATE] SUCCESS: encoder initialized, frame_count=0, fps=%d, dims=%dx%d", enc->fps, enc->width_px, enc->height_px);
641   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
642 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx, ERROR_INIT,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ffmpeg_encoder_s::estimated_frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::estimated_frame_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::first_frame_captured_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame, ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::height_px, ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_image,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_stdout_pipe, log_debug, log_info,
    LOG_IO, NAMED_REGISTER_FFMPEG_ENCODER,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt, SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SET_ERRNO, ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_elapsed_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::target_pix_fmt, ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts,
    and ffmpeg_encoder_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_create().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_destroy
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc)


Close and finalize the output file, releasing all encoder
    resources.




This function flushes any pending frames in the encoder, writes
    the file trailer, and releases all allocated resources (memory buffers, file
    handles, codecs).




Critical: This MUST be called to properly finalize the
    output file. Without this call, the file may be incomplete or
  unplayable.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success (currently always succeeds)


Note


Safe to call with NULL pointer (returns ASCIICHAT_OK).



After this call, enc must not be used again.




The output file is NOT guaranteed to be complete until this
    function returns.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create


Definition at line 894 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


894                                                                 {
895   if (!enc)
896     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
897 
898   NAMED_UNREGISTER(enc);
899 
900   // In snapshot mode, calculate the FPS needed to match the target duration
901   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
902   int64_t adjusted_packet_duration = 0;
903   double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
904 
905   if (snapshot_mode && enc->frame_count > 0 && snapshot_delay > 0) {
906     // Calculate output FPS that distributes frames across snapshot_delay seconds
907     // output_fps = frame_count / snapshot_delay
908     // packet_duration (in stream time_base) = stream_time_base.den / output_fps
909     //                                        = (stream_time_base.den * snapshot_delay) / frame_count
910     adjusted_packet_duration = (int64_t)((snapshot_delay * enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->frame_count);
911     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Snapshot mode - adjusting packet durations for %d frames over %.2f seconds",
912              enc->frame_count, snapshot_delay);
913     log_debug("  Adjusted packet duration: %lld units (%.6f seconds per frame)",
914               (long long)adjusted_packet_duration,
915               (double)adjusted_packet_duration / enc->stream->time_base.den);
916   }
917 
918   // Flush video encoder (capture libx264 final statistics)
919   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
920     avcodec_send_frame(enc->codec_ctx, NULL);
921     int flush_pkt_count = 0;
922     while (1) {
923       int ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
924       if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
925         break;
926       if (ret < 0)
927         break;
928 
929       flush_pkt_count++;
930 
931       // Set packet duration in codec time base if not already set
932       if (enc->pkt->duration == 0) {
933         enc->pkt->duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
934       }
935 
936       // In snapshot mode, rescale to stream time_base and apply adjusted duration if set
937       int64_t duration_before = enc->pkt->duration;
938       av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->codec_ctx->time_base, enc->stream->time_base);
939       if (snapshot_mode && adjusted_packet_duration > 0) {
940         enc->pkt->duration = adjusted_packet_duration;
941         log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Flush packet %d duration adjusted from %lld to %lld",
942                   flush_pkt_count, (long long)duration_before, (long long)adjusted_packet_duration);
943       }
944       enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->stream->index;
945       av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
946       av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
947     }
948   });
949 
950   // Flush audio encoder if present
951   if (enc->has_audio_stream && enc->audio_codec_ctx) {
952     // Write any remaining partial audio samples as padding
953     if (enc->audio_partial_len > 0) {
954       // Zero-pad the remaining samples
955       memset(&enc->audio_partial_buf[enc->audio_partial_len], 0,
956              (enc->audio_frame_size - enc->audio_partial_len) * sizeof(float));
957       enc->audio_frame->data[0] = (uint8_t *)enc->audio_partial_buf;
958       enc->audio_frame->linesize[0] = enc->audio_frame_size * sizeof(float);
959       enc->audio_frame->nb_samples = enc->audio_frame_size;
960       enc->audio_frame->pts = enc->audio_pts;
961       avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->audio_frame);
962     }
963 
964     // Flush any remaining packets (capture FFmpeg audio codec logs)
965     LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
966       avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, NULL);
967       while (1) {
968         int ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
969         if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
970           break;
971         if (ret < 0)
972           break;
973 
974         av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->audio_codec_ctx->time_base, enc->audio_stream->time_base);
975         enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->audio_stream->index;
976         av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
977         av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
978       }
979     });
980   }
981 
982   // Set stream duration for proper metadata (must be before trailer)
983   if (enc->stream && enc->frame_count > 0) {
984     int64_t duration;
985     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
986     if (snapshot_mode) {
987       extern uint64_t g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms;
988       double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
989       // Use actual capture elapsed time if available, otherwise use snapshot_delay
990       // This ensures frames are distributed across the correct time window
991       double actual_capture_duration = g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms / 1000.0;
992       double effective_duration = (actual_capture_duration > 0) ? actual_capture_duration : snapshot_delay;
993 
994       // In snapshot mode with wall-clock timing, the video duration should equal the actual elapsed time
995       // duration_seconds = effective_duration
996       // duration_time_base_units = effective_duration * time_base.den
997       duration = (int64_t)(effective_duration * enc->stream->time_base.den);
998 
999       // Calculate what the frame durations should be to sum to effective_duration
1000       // This ensures FFmpeg plays the captured frames across the full effective_duration
1001       // effective_frame_duration = effective_duration / frame_count (seconds per frame)
1002       int64_t effective_frame_duration = (int64_t)((effective_duration / (double)enc->frame_count) * enc->stream->time_base.den);
1003 
1004       log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Snapshot mode - snapshot_delay=%.2f, frames=%d, output_duration=%.2f sec",
1005                snapshot_delay, enc->frame_count, effective_duration);
1006       log_debug("  Using effective_duration=%.2f seconds for output", effective_duration);
1007       log_debug("  stream->time_base=%d/%d, effective frame duration=%lld time_base units",
1008                 enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den, (long long)effective_frame_duration);
1009       log_debug("  (each frame should span %.3f seconds to total %.2f seconds)",
1010                 effective_duration / (double)enc->frame_count, effective_duration);
1011 
1012       log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Setting stream->duration=%lld (effective_duration=%.3f, time_base=%d/%d, equiv=%.3f sec)",
1013                (long long)duration, effective_duration,
1014                enc->stream->time_base.num, enc->stream->time_base.den,
1015                (double)duration * enc->stream->time_base.num / enc->stream->time_base.den);
1016     } else {
1017       // Normal mode: calculate duration from frame count and FPS
1018       // time_base = 1 / time_base.den seconds per unit
1019       // Each frame is 1/fps seconds, so duration = frame_count / fps seconds
1020       // In time base units: duration = (frame_count / fps) * time_base.den = frame_count * time_base.den / fps
1021       duration = (int64_t)enc->frame_count * enc->stream->time_base.den / enc->fps;
1022       log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Normal mode - Set stream duration=%lld (frames=%d, fps=%d, time_base=%d/%d)",
1023                 (long long)duration, enc->frame_count, enc->fps, 1, enc->stream->time_base.den);
1024     }
1025     enc->stream->duration = duration;
1026 
1027     // CRITICAL: Adjust last frame duration to ensure total duration matches stream->duration
1028     // Each frame was encoded with FPS-based duration, but snapshot mode needs them distributed
1029     // across the actual capture duration. Since we can't change past frame durations, we extend
1030     // the last frame's duration to make the sum equal the desired total.
1031     if (snapshot_mode) {
1032       // Calculate sum of all frame durations (each frame currently has the same FPS-based duration)
1033       int64_t current_total_duration = enc->video_pts;  // This is the sum of all frame durations so far
1034       int64_t desired_duration = duration;
1035 
1036       // The last frame needs to be extended to: desired - (sum of all other frames)
1037       // This ensures the video plays for exactly the snapshot_delay duration
1038       int64_t last_frame_extension = desired_duration - current_total_duration;
1039 
1040       if (last_frame_extension > 0) {
1041         // We need to extend the last frame. But we can't modify it directly after encoding.
1042         // Instead, the muxer will use stream->duration as the authoritative source.
1043         log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Last frame would need extension of %lld units to reach total %lld (currently %lld)",
1044                   (long long)last_frame_extension, (long long)desired_duration, (long long)current_total_duration);
1045       }
1046     }
1047   }
1048 
1049   // Write trailer (capture FFmpeg muxer logs and final frame statistics)
1050   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1051     av_write_trailer(enc->fmt_ctx);
1052   });
1053 
1054   // Flush output buffer for stdout/pipes before closing
1055   if (enc->fmt_ctx && enc->fmt_ctx->pb) {
1056     avio_flush(enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
1057     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: Flushed output buffer");
1058   }
1059 
1060   // Close output file
1061   if (enc->fmt_ctx && !(enc->fmt_ctx->oformat->flags & AVFMT_NOFILE))
1062     avio_closep(&enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
1063 
1064   // Cleanup
1065   sws_freeContext(enc->sws_ctx);
1066   av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
1067   av_frame_free(&enc->frame_encoded);
1068 
1069   // Cleanup audio resources (capture any remaining FFmpeg logs from codec cleanup)
1070   if (enc->has_audio_stream) {
1071     swr_free(&enc->audio_swr_ctx);
1072     av_frame_free(&enc->audio_frame);
1073     LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1074       avcodec_free_context(&enc->audio_codec_ctx);
1075     });
1076     SAFE_FREE(enc->audio_partial_buf);
1077   }
1078 
1079   av_packet_free(&enc->pkt);
1080   LOG_IO("ffmpeg", {
1081     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
1082   });
1083   if (enc->fmt_ctx)
1084     avformat_free_context(enc->fmt_ctx);
1085 
1086   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_destroy: wrote %d frames", enc->frame_count);
1087   SAFE_FREE(enc);
1088   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1089 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_codec_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame_size,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_buf,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_len,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_pts, ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_stream,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_swr_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx,
    EAGAIN, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps, ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded,
    g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::has_audio_stream, log_debug,
    log_info, LOG_IO, NAMED_UNREGISTER,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt, SAFE_FREE,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx, and
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts.


Referenced by render_file_destroy().






void
  ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc,
  double actual_duration_sec)


Set the actual wall-clock duration for snapshot mode frame
  timing.




In snapshot mode, frames are captured over a wall-clock duration
    (e.g., 3 seconds of real time). This function should be called after all
    frames are captured to provide the actual elapsed time, allowing the encoder
    to calculate correct frame durations so the output video duration matches
    the actual capture time.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


actual_duration_sec Actual wall-clock duration in seconds (e.g., 3.5 for
  3.5 seconds)


Note


Only used in snapshot mode. In normal mode, this is
  ignored.



Should be called before ffmpeg_encoder_destroy() for the
    adjustment to take effect.




If not called, frame durations are calculated from FPS, which may
    not match actual wall-clock capture time.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_destroy


Definition at line 875 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


875                                                                                                     {
876   if (!enc)
877     return;
878   enc->snapshot_actual_duration = actual_duration_sec;
879 
880   // If we already have frame count, we can calculate the adjusted packet duration now
881   // This will be used for any remaining frames and during flush
882   if (enc->frame_count > 0 && actual_duration_sec > 0) {
883     // Calculate the packet duration that would make all frames fit in actual_duration_sec
884     // packet_duration_units = (actual_duration_sec * time_base.den) / frame_count
885     // This is only a starting point - the actual duration might be different if more frames are captured
886     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration: %.3f sec with %d frames so far",
887               actual_duration_sec, enc->frame_count);
888   } else {
889     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_set_snapshot_actual_duration: Setting duration to %.3f seconds (already encoded %d frames)",
890             actual_duration_sec, enc->frame_count);
891   }
892 }






References ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count, log_debug,
    log_info, and ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration.


Referenced by
  render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc, const float * samples, int num_samples)


Write audio samples to the output file.




Encodes and writes audio samples to the output file. Audio samples
    must be in 48kHz mono float32 format. The function internally buffers and
    accumulates samples into codec frames before encoding.




Audio Format Requirements:


    
  
  • Sample rate: 48000 Hz (48 kHz)
    • 
  
  • Channels: Mono (1 channel)
    • 
  
  • Format: 32-bit IEEE float (AV_SAMPLE_FMT_FLT)
    • 



Notes:


    
  
  • Audio is only written if the output format supports audio (MP4, WebM,
      AVI).
    • 
  
  • Image formats (PNG, JPEG, GIF) ignore audio writes.
    • 
  
  • Samples are buffered internally and encoded in chunks as needed.
    • 
  
  • Timing is automatically synchronized with video using PTS (presentation
      timestamp).
    • 



Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


samples Pointer to audio samples in float32 format (not owned, used
  immediately)


num_samples Number of samples to write (must be > 0)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Note


Input sample data is NOT owned by the encoder. The caller
  must ensure the buffer remains valid throughout the call.



Safe to call on encoders without audio streams (no-op).




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame


Definition at line 808 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


808                                                                                                            {
809   if (!enc || !samples || num_samples <= 0)
810     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Silently ignore invalid calls
811 
812   // No audio stream for this format
813   if (!enc->has_audio_stream)
814     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
815 
816   // Accumulate samples into partial buffer
817   int samples_to_process = num_samples;
818   int samples_offset = 0;
819 
820   while (samples_to_process > 0) {
821     // How many samples can we add to the partial buffer?
822     int space_in_buffer = enc->audio_frame_size - enc->audio_partial_len;
823     int samples_to_copy = (samples_to_process < space_in_buffer) ? samples_to_process : space_in_buffer;
824 
825     // Copy samples to partial buffer
826     memcpy(&enc->audio_partial_buf[enc->audio_partial_len], &samples[samples_offset], samples_to_copy * sizeof(float));
827     enc->audio_partial_len += samples_to_copy;
828     samples_offset += samples_to_copy;
829     samples_to_process -= samples_to_copy;
830 
831     // If buffer is full, encode and write frame
832     if (enc->audio_partial_len >= enc->audio_frame_size) {
833       // Set up audio frame with accumulated samples
834       enc->audio_frame->data[0] = (uint8_t *)enc->audio_partial_buf;
835       enc->audio_frame->linesize[0] = enc->audio_frame_size * sizeof(float);
836       enc->audio_frame->nb_samples = enc->audio_frame_size;
837       enc->audio_frame->pts = enc->audio_pts;
838 
839       // Send frame to encoder
840       int ret = avcodec_send_frame(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->audio_frame);
841       if (ret < 0) {
842         log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_send_frame for audio failed");
843       } else {
844         // Receive and write packets
845         while (ret >= 0) {
846           ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->audio_codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
847           if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
848             break;
849           if (ret < 0) {
850             log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_receive_packet for audio failed");
851             break;
852           }
853 
854           // Rescale timestamp and write
855           av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->audio_codec_ctx->time_base, enc->audio_stream->time_base);
856           enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->audio_stream->index;
857           ret = av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
858           av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
859           if (ret < 0) {
860             log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: av_interleaved_write_frame for audio failed");
861             break;
862           }
863         }
864       }
865 
866       // Update PTS for next frame
867       enc->audio_pts += enc->audio_frame_size;
868       enc->audio_partial_len = 0;
869     }
870   }
871 
872   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
873 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_codec_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_frame_size,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_buf,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_partial_len,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_pts, ffmpeg_encoder_s::audio_stream,
    EAGAIN, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::has_audio_stream, log_warn_every, and
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().






asciichat_error_t ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame
  (ffmpeg_encoder_t * enc, const uint8_t * rgb, int pitch,
  uint64_t captured_ns)


Write a single video frame to the output file.




The frame must be in RGB24 format (8-bit per channel, 24-bit per
    pixel). Internal color space conversion is performed automatically based on
    the output format (see Encoder Options & Quality Tradeoffs for
    details).




Pitch Parameter: The pitch parameter specifies the byte
    offset from one row to the next. For tightly-packed RGB data: pitch =
    width_px × 3. For padded/aligned buffers: pitch must reflect the
    actual row size.




Timestamp Parameter: The captured_ns parameter is the
    wall-clock timestamp (in nanoseconds) when the frame was captured. This
    ensures video playback uses actual capture timing, not encoder processing
    time. For correct frame timing in the output video, captured_ns should be
    the time when the frame was acquired from the media source.




Parameters


enc Encoder handle (created with
  ffmpeg_encoder_create())


rgb Pointer to frame data in RGB24 format (not copied, used immediately)


pitch Byte offset between rows (must match actual buffer layout)


captured_ns Wall-clock timestamp in nanoseconds when frame was
  captured


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Note


Input frame data is NOT owned by the encoder. The caller
  must ensure the buffer remains valid throughout the call.



For image-only formats (PNG, JPEG), only the last frame written is
    kept. Previous frames are discarded when the next frame is written.




See also


ffmpeg_encoder_create,
  ffmpeg_encoder_destroy


Definition at line 644 of file ffmpeg_encoder.c.


645                                                                    {
646   if (!enc || !rgb)
647     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: NULL input");
648 
649   // Initialize first frame capture time on first call
650   if (enc->first_frame_captured_ns == 0) {
651     enc->first_frame_captured_ns = captured_ns;
652     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: first frame captured at %llu ns", (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
653   }
654 
655   log_info("[FFMPEG_ENCODER_WRITE] frame %d, pitch=%d, dims=%dx%d", enc->frame_count, pitch, enc->width_px, enc->height_px);
656   log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: frame %d, pitch=%d, dims=%dx%d, captured_ns=%llu", enc->frame_count, pitch, enc->width_px,
657             enc->height_px, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
658 
659   // Set up RGB frame for conversion
660   uint8_t *data[1] = {(uint8_t *)rgb};
661   int linesize[1] = {pitch};
662 
663   // CRITICAL: Ensure frame format is set before each sws_scale call
664   // Some FFmpeg versions reset the format field, so we need to set it explicitly
665   enc->frame_encoded->format = enc->target_pix_fmt;
666 
667   // Convert RGB24 to target format
668   sws_scale(enc->sws_ctx, (const uint8_t *const *)data, linesize, 0, enc->height_px, enc->frame_encoded->data,
669             enc->frame_encoded->linesize);
670 
671   // Set frame duration based on mode
672   int64_t frame_duration;
673   bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
674 
675   if (enc->frame_count == 0) {
676     log_info("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: frame 0, snapshot_mode=%d", snapshot_mode);
677   }
678 
679   // Use FPS-based frame duration (snapshot mode adjusts total duration in destroy())
680   frame_duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
681 
682   enc->frame_encoded->duration = frame_duration;
683 
684   // Track this frame's capture timestamp for duration calculation of next frame
685   if (snapshot_mode) {
686     uint64_t frame_dur_ms = (enc->previous_captured_ns > 0) ? (captured_ns - enc->previous_captured_ns) / 1000000 : 0;
687     if (enc->frame_count <= 2) {
688       log_info("ffmpeg: frame %d SET duration=%lld units (%.3fms since last, codec_base=1/%d)",
689                enc->frame_count, (long long)frame_duration, (double)frame_dur_ms,
690                enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den);
691     }
692     enc->previous_captured_ns = captured_ns;
693   }
694 
695   // Calculate PTS from actual capture timestamp
696   // time_base is {1, fps}, so 1 PTS unit = 1/fps seconds
697   // Convert elapsed nanoseconds to PTS units: elapsed_ns * fps / 1_000_000_000
698   uint64_t elapsed_ns = captured_ns - enc->first_frame_captured_ns;
699   int64_t pts_from_timestamp = (int64_t)((elapsed_ns * (uint64_t)enc->fps) / 1000000000ULL);
700 
701   // In snapshot mode, distribute frames linearly across the actual duration
702   if (snapshot_mode) {
703     extern uint64_t g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms;
704     extern uint64_t g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns;
705     if (g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms > 0) {
706       // Distribute frames linearly based on their capture timestamp relative to total duration
707       // pts = (frame_elapsed_ns / total_elapsed_ns) * actual_duration_sec * fps
708       // This ensures frame 0 is at 0s and last frame is at actual_duration_sec
709       double actual_duration_sec = (double)g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms / 1000.0;
710       double total_elapsed_sec = (double)g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT;
711 
712       if (total_elapsed_sec > 0.001) {
713         double frame_fraction = (double)elapsed_ns / (double)g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns;
714         double target_pts_sec = frame_fraction * actual_duration_sec;
715         int64_t pts_before = pts_from_timestamp;
716         // Convert target seconds to time_base units: pts = target_sec * time_base.den / time_base.num
717         pts_from_timestamp = (int64_t)(target_pts_sec * (double)enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (double)enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num);
718 
719         if (enc->frame_count < 3 || enc->frame_count % 10 == 0) {
720           log_debug("ffmpeg: snapshot frame %d: PTS distributed (before=%lld, after=%lld, sec=%lld->%lld, frac=%.3f)",
721                     enc->frame_count, (long long)pts_before, (long long)pts_from_timestamp,
722                     (long long)((double)pts_before * enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num / enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den),
723                     (long long)target_pts_sec, frame_fraction);
724         }
725       }
726     }
727   }
728 
729   // Use timestamp-based (or scaled) PTS
730   enc->frame_encoded->pts = pts_from_timestamp;
731   enc->video_pts += frame_duration;
732 
733   if (!snapshot_mode) {
734     log_debug("ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame: PTS calculation - elapsed_ns=%llu, pts_from_timestamp=%lld, fps=%d",
735               (unsigned long long)elapsed_ns, (long long)pts_from_timestamp, enc->fps);
736   }
737 
738   // Send frame to encoder
739   int ret = avcodec_send_frame(enc->codec_ctx, enc->frame_encoded);
740   if (ret < 0) {
741     log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_send_frame failed");
742     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Continue anyway
743   }
744 
745   // Receive packets from encoder and write to file
746   static int write_frame_pkt_count = 0;
747 
748   while (ret >= 0) {
749     ret = avcodec_receive_packet(enc->codec_ctx, enc->pkt);
750     if (ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN) || ret == AVERROR_EOF)
751       break;
752     if (ret < 0) {
753       log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: avcodec_receive_packet failed");
754       break;
755     }
756 
757     write_frame_pkt_count++;
758 
759     // Set packet duration in codec time base (duration of one frame at target FPS)
760     // This ensures proper stream duration calculation
761     if (enc->pkt->duration == 0) {
762       enc->pkt->duration = enc->codec_ctx->time_base.den / (enc->codec_ctx->time_base.num * enc->fps);
763     }
764 
765     av_packet_rescale_ts(enc->pkt, enc->codec_ctx->time_base, enc->stream->time_base);
766     enc->pkt->stream_index = enc->stream->index;
767 
768     // In snapshot mode, apply adjusted duration if snapshot_actual_duration is set
769     // This happens when we get the actual duration from the capture thread
770     bool is_snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
771     if (is_snapshot_mode && enc->snapshot_actual_duration > 0 && enc->frame_count > 0) {
772       int64_t adjusted_dur = (int64_t)((enc->snapshot_actual_duration * enc->stream->time_base.den) / enc->frame_count);
773       // Only apply if it's significantly different from the default
774       if (adjusted_dur > enc->pkt->duration * 2) {
775         enc->pkt->duration = adjusted_dur;
776         log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame packet %d duration adjusted to %lld units based on actual duration",
777                   write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration);
778       }
779     } else if (!is_snapshot_mode && write_frame_pkt_count < 3) {
780       log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame pkt %d, duration=%lld, snapshot_mode=%d, actual_dur=%.2f, frame_count=%d",
781                 write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration, is_snapshot_mode, enc->snapshot_actual_duration,
782                 enc->frame_count);
783     }
784 
785     if (write_frame_pkt_count <= 2 || write_frame_pkt_count >= 16) {
786       log_debug("ffmpeg: write_frame pkt %d with final duration=%lld (%.6f sec in stream time_base), total frames=%d",
787                 write_frame_pkt_count, (long long)enc->pkt->duration,
788                 (double)enc->pkt->duration / enc->stream->time_base.den, enc->frame_count);
789     }
790 
791     ret = av_interleaved_write_frame(enc->fmt_ctx, enc->pkt);
792     av_packet_unref(enc->pkt);
793     if (ret < 0) {
794       log_warn_every(5 * 1000000000LL, "ffmpeg: av_interleaved_write_frame failed");
795       break;
796     }
797 
798     // Flush output buffer for stdout to ensure frames are written immediately
799     if (enc->is_stdout_pipe) {
800       avio_flush(enc->fmt_ctx->pb);
801     }
802   }
803 
804   enc->frame_count++;
805   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
806 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::codec_ctx, EAGAIN,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::first_frame_captured_ns,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::fmt_ctx, ffmpeg_encoder_s::fps,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_count,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::frame_encoded,
    g_snapshot_actual_duration_ms,
    g_snapshot_last_capture_elapsed_ns, GET_OPTION,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::height_px, ffmpeg_encoder_s::is_stdout_pipe,
    log_debug, log_info, log_warn_every,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, ffmpeg_encoder_s::pkt,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::previous_captured_ns, SET_ERRNO,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::snapshot_actual_duration,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::stream, ffmpeg_encoder_s::sws_ctx,
    ffmpeg_encoder_s::target_pix_fmt, ffmpeg_encoder_s::video_pts,
    and ffmpeg_encoder_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_write_frame().

    Version 0.11.0
